what: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: Compares fitted bayesma models using LOSO-CV (leave-one-study-cross-validation) primary ranking criterion, plus within-stage LOO-CV diagnostics. LOSO-CV evaluates models question — "predict held-study" — effect-size scale, making valid comparing across one-stage two-stage models.

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
code: compare_models(   ...,   data = NULL,   studyvar = NULL,   criterion = c("loso", "loo"),   loso = TRUE,   coverage_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.9, 0.95),   moment_match = FALSE,   cores = getOption("mc.cores", 1),   quiet = FALSE )

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: ... Named unnamed bayesma objects compare. unnamed, models labeled "Model 1", "Model 2", etc. data original data frame used fit models. Required LOSO-CV (default). studyvar Column identifying studies data (unquoted). Required LOSO-CV. criterion Character. Primary criterion ranking. "loso" (default): leave-one-study-CRPS effect-size scale. Comparable across stages. "loo": within-stage LOO-CV . loso Logical. Run LOSO-CV. Default TRUE. Set FALSE fast within-stage-comparison. coverage_levels Numeric vector. Nominal coverage levels calibration assessment LOSO-CV. Default: c(0.50, 0.80, 0.90, 0.95). moment_match Logical. Attempt moment matching LOO-CV. Default FALSE. cores Integer. Number cores LOO computation. Default getOption("mc.cores", 1). quiet Logical. Suppress progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: object class "bayesma_comparison" containing: comparison Tibble comparison metrics loso_list List LOSO-CV results per model (computed) loo_compare List loo::loo_compare() per stage group loo_list List LOO objects per model waic_list List WAIC objects per model diagnostics LOO diagnostics (Pareto k) model_names Character vector model names

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: LOO-CV WAIC operate model's native likelihood. One-stage models condition arm-level counts; two-stage models condition summary effect sizes. likelihoods different scales, ELPD values comparable across stages. LOSO-CV sidesteps asking model question: "Given studies except study s, predictive distribution study s's effect size?" model types answer scale (effect-size scale), making comparison valid.

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: LOSO-CRPS Continuous Ranked Probability Score averaged held-studies. proper scoring rule evaluates full predictive distribution. Lower better. LOSO-ELPD Mean log predictive density held-study. Higher better. Analogous LOO-ELPD truly --sample. LOSO-Coverage nominal level, proportion held-yi fall inside corresponding prediction interval? Well-calibrated models empirical ≈ nominal. LOSO-Miscalibration Mean |empirical − nominal| across coverage levels. Zero perfect.

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: LOO-CV still computed within-stage diagnostics (Pareto k values identify influential studies) within-stage ranking models share stage.

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
text: LOSO-CV refits model S times (per study). M models S studies, M × S fits. Set loso = FALSE fast within-stage-comparison, use quiet = TRUE suppress per-fold messages.

title: Compare Multiple Bayesian Meta-Analysis Models — compare_models
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ comparison <- compare_models(   "RE (two-stage)" = mod_2s_re,   "RE (one-stage)"  = mod_1s_re,   "Heavy-tailed"    = mod_2s_t,   data = dat,   studyvar = author ) print(comparison) compare_plot(comparison, type = "loso_crps") compare_plot(comparison, type = "calibration") compare_table(comparison)  # Fast within-stage only (no refitting) compare_models(mod1, mod2, criterion = "loo", loso = FALSE) } # }

what: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
title: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
text: Creates empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot comparing posterior distributions across different model strategies (e.g., random effects, selection models, PET-PEESE) single prior specification. Use plot assess robust conclusions across different modelling assumptions.

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
code: ecdf_model_plot(   model,   data,   prior,   estimand,   rob_var = NULL,   exclude_high_rob = FALSE,   incl_common_effect = FALSE,   incl_random_effect = TRUE,   incl_bias_corrected = FALSE,   incl_selection_copas = FALSE,   incl_selection_weight = FALSE,   incl_pet_peese = FALSE,   incl_robust = FALSE,   incl_robma = FALSE,   prob_reference = "null",   null_value = NULL,   null_range = NULL,   add_null_range = FALSE,   color_null_range = "#77bb41",   label_control = "Control",   label_intervention = "Intervention",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlim = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   color_palette = NULL,   show_density = TRUE,   font = NULL )

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
text: model fitted bayesma object. data data frame containing study data used fit model. prior list containing prior specification elements: mu_prior (Required) Prior specification mu. tau_prior (Optional) Prior specification tau. name Display label prior. Required incl_robma = TRUE used match priors model$robma_sensitivity. provided incl_robma = FALSE, defaults "User-specified prior". estimand Effect estimand string (e.g., "", "RR", "HR", "IRR", "MD", "SMD"). rob_var Optional. Name risk--bias variable (unquoted). exclude_high_rob Logical. TRUE, includes "Excluding High RoB" section. incl_common_effect Logical. Include common effect model. Default FALSE. incl_random_effect Logical. Include random effects model. Default TRUE. incl_bias_corrected Logical. Include bias-corrected model. Default FALSE. incl_selection_copas Logical. Include Copas selection model. Default FALSE. incl_selection_weight Logical. Include weight-function selection model. Default FALSE. incl_pet_peese Logical. Include PET-PEESE model. Default FALSE. incl_robust Logical. Include robust mixture model. Default FALSE. incl_robma Logical. Include RoBMA model. Requires model$robma_sensitivity present prior$name match one priors used fitting. Default FALSE. prob_reference Character string. reference use probability axis labels. Either "null" (compare null_value) "null_range" (compare null_range boundaries). Default "null". null_value Null hypothesis value. NULL, uses estimand default. null_range Numeric vector length 2 giving null range bounds. add_null_range Logical. TRUE null_range NULL, uses estimand-appropriate defaults. color_null_range Fill colour null range band. Default "#77bb41". label_control Label control group. Default "Control". label_intervention Label intervention group. Default "Intervention". title Optional plot title. subtitle Optional plot subtitle. NULL prior name available, displays prior name. xlim Optional x-axis limits. x_breaks Optional x-axis break points. color_palette Optional named vector colours model sections. show_density Logical. TRUE, includes density plot ECDF. Default TRUE. font Optional font family.

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
text: ggplot object (patchwork object show_density = TRUE).

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
text: plot shows one ECDF line per model strategy, using prior. allows direct comparison different modelling assumptions (random effects, selection models, bias correction, etc.) affect posterior distribution. left y-axis shows P(effect < x) right y-axis shows P(effect > x).

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Model Strategies — ecdf_model_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ priors <- list(   vague = list(     name = "Vague",     mu_prior = normal(0, 10),     tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 1)   ),   weak_reg = list(     name = "Weakly Regularising",     mu_prior = normal(0, 1),     tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 0.5)   ) )  ecdf_model_plot(   model = model,   data = dat,   estimand = "OR",   prior = priors$weak_reg,   incl_random_effect = TRUE,   incl_pet_peese = TRUE )  ecdf_model_plot(   model = model,   data = dat,   estimand = "OR",   prior = priors$weak_reg,   incl_robma = TRUE ) } # }

code: ecdf_plot(   model,   data,   priors,   measure,   study_var = NULL,   rob_var = NULL,   exclude_high_rob = FALSE,   incl_common_effect = FALSE,   incl_random_effect = TRUE,   incl_bias_corrected = FALSE,   incl_selection_copas = FALSE,   incl_selection_weight = FALSE,   incl_pet_peese = FALSE,   pet_peese_direction = "negative",   pet_peese_threshold = 0.1,   incl_robust = FALSE,   incl_mixture = FALSE,   incl_bma = FALSE,   model_bma = NULL,   prob_reference = "null",   null_value = NULL,   null_range = NULL,   add_null_range = FALSE,   color_null_range = "#77bb41",   label_control = "Control",   label_intervention = "Intervention",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlim = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   color_palette = NULL,   show_density = TRUE,   prior_to_plot = "weakreg",   font = NULL )

title: Create ECDF Plot for Sensitivity Analysis — ecdf_plot
text: model fitted bayesma object. RoBMA results, must $robma_sensitivity attached via attach_robma_sensitivity(). data data frame containing study data used fit model. priors named list prior specifications. element must list least mu_prior (optionally) tau_prior, may include name used display. measure Effect measure string (e.g., "", "RR", "HR", "IRR", "MD", "SMD"). rob_var Optional. Name risk--bias variable (unquoted). exclude_high_rob Logical. TRUE, runs "Excluding High RoB" section. incl_common_effect, incl_random_effect, incl_bias_corrected, incl_selection_copas, incl_selection_weight, incl_pet_peese, incl_robust Logical. model strategy sections include. prob_reference Character string. reference use probability calculations. Either "null" (compare null_value) "null_range" (compare null_range boundaries). Default "null". null_value, null_range, add_null_range, color_null_range ROPE settings. label_control, label_intervention Group labels plot. xlim, x_breaks Density axis settings. show_density Logical. TRUE, includes density plot ECDF. Default TRUE. prior_to_plot Character string. prior specification plot ECDF. One "vague", "weakreg", "informative". Default "weakreg". font Optional font family.

title: Create ECDF Plot for Sensitivity Analysis — ecdf_plot
text: ggplot object (patchwork object show_density = TRUE).

title: Create ECDF Plot for Sensitivity Analysis — ecdf_plot
text: output ECDF plot includes left y-axis showing P(effect < x) right y-axis showing P(effect > x), optional ROPE shading optional density panel .

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Prior Sensitivity — ecdf_prior_plot
text: model fitted bayesma object. data data frame containing study data used fit model. priors named list prior specifications. element must list least mu_prior optionally tau_prior, may include name display labels. estimand Effect estimand string (e.g., "", "RR", "HR", "IRR", "MD", "SMD"). model_types Character vector specifying model type(s) include. Maximum 2 model types avoid clutter. Valid values: "common_effect", "random_effect", "bias_corrected", "selection_copas", "selection_weight", "pet_peese", "robust", "robma". Default "random_effect". prior_order Optional character vector specifying display order priors. contain names (IDs) priors desired order. NULL, priors displayed order appear priors. prob_reference Character string. reference use probability axis labels. Either "null" (compare null_value) "null_range" (compare null_range boundaries). Default "null". null_value Null hypothesis value. NULL, uses estimand default. null_range Numeric vector length 2 giving null range bounds. add_null_range Logical. TRUE null_range NULL, uses estimand-appropriate defaults. color_null_range Fill colour null range band. Default "#77bb41". label_control Label control group. Default "Control". label_intervention Label intervention group. Default "Intervention". title Optional plot title. subtitle Optional plot subtitle. NULL single model type selected, displays model type. xlim Optional x-axis limits. x_breaks Optional x-axis break points. color_palette Optional named vector colours priors. linetype_by_model Logical. TRUE multiple model types selected, uses different linetypes model type. Default TRUE. show_density Logical. TRUE, includes density plot ECDF. Default TRUE. font Optional font family.

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Prior Sensitivity — ecdf_prior_plot
text: ggplot object (patchwork object show_density = TRUE).

title: ECDF Plot Comparing Prior Sensitivity — ecdf_prior_plot
text: plot shows one ECDF line per prior specification (per model type multiple selected). allows direct comparison different prior choices affect posterior distribution. two model types selected, colour represents prior linetype represents model type, allowing comparison dimensions simultaneously. left y-axis shows P(effect < x) right y-axis shows P(effect > x).

what: Create Bayesian Forest Plot for Meta-Analysis — forest
title: Create Bayesian Forest Plot for Meta-Analysis — forest
text: function creates Bayesian forest plot meta-analysis bayesma model object.

title: Create Bayesian Forest Plot for Meta-Analysis — forest
code: forest(   model,   data,   estimand,   studyvar = NULL,   year = NULL,   c_n = NULL,   i_n = NULL,   c_event = NULL,   i_event = NULL,   c_mean = NULL,   i_mean = NULL,   c_sd = NULL,   i_sd = NULL,   c_time = NULL,   i_time = NULL,   sort_studies_by = "author",   subgroup = FALSE,   subgroup_var = NULL,   sort_subgroup_by = "alphabetical",   label_outcome = "Outcome",   label_control = "Control",   label_intervention = "Intervention",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   title_align = "left",   xlim = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   add_rope = FALSE,   rope_value = NULL,   rope_color = "grey50",   shrinkage_output = "density",   null_value = NULL,   color_palette = NULL,   color_study_posterior_null_left = "deepskyblue",   color_study_posterior_null_right = "violet",   color_study_posterior = "dodgerblue",   color_study_posterior_outline = "blue",   color_overall_posterior = "blue",   color_shrinkage_pointinterval = "purple",   color_shrinkage_outline = "purple",   color_shrinkage_fill = NULL,   split_color_by_null = FALSE,   color_favours_control = "firebrick",   color_favours_intervention = "dodgerblue",   add_arm_labels = TRUE,   reverse_arm_labels = FALSE,   add_pred = FALSE,   add_pred_subgroup = FALSE,   pred_output = c("density", "pointinterval"),   color_pred_posterior = "orange",   color_pred_outline = "darkorange2",   color_pred_pointinterval = "orange",   plot_width = 4,   add_rob = FALSE,   rob_tool = c("rob2", "robins_i", "quadas2", "robins_e"),   add_rob_legend = FALSE,   exclude_high_rob = FALSE,   re_min_k = NULL,   incl_shrinkage = TRUE,   font = NULL )

title: Create Bayesian Forest Plot for Meta-Analysis — forest
text: model fitted bayesma object (class 'bayesma'). data data frame containing study data used meta-analysis. estimand Character string specifying effect measure marginal estimand. Must one : Relative-effect: "" (Odds Ratio), "HR" (Hazard Ratio), "RR" (Risk Ratio), "IRR" (Incidence Rate Ratio), "MD" (Mean Difference), "SMD" (Standardised Mean Difference). Marginal: "RD" / "ARR" (Risk Difference), "ATE" (Average Treatment Effect), "ATT" (Average Treatment Effect Treated), "CATE" (Conditional Average Treatment Effect). studyvar Column name containing study identifiers/authors. Default NULL. Extracted model object provided. year Column name containing publication years. Default NULL. c_n Column name containing control group sample sizes. Required , RR, MD, SMD. i_n Column name containing intervention group sample sizes. Required , RR, MD, SMD. c_event Column name containing control group event counts. Required , RR, IRR. i_event Column name containing intervention group event counts. Required , RR, IRR. c_mean Column name containing control group means. Required MD, SMD. i_mean Column name containing intervention group means. Required MD, SMD. c_sd Column name containing control group standard deviations. Required MD, SMD. i_sd Column name containing intervention group standard deviations. Required MD, SMD. c_time Column name containing control group time periods. Required IRR. i_time Column name containing intervention group time periods. Required IRR. sort_studies_by Character string specifying sort studies. Options: "author" (default), "year", "effect". subgroup Logical indicating whether create subgroup analysis. Default FALSE. subgroup_var Character string. Name variable data use subgroup analysis. sort_subgroup_by Character string vector specifying subgroup ordering. Options: "alphabetical" (default), "effect", custom character vector subgroup names. label_outcome Character string outcome label. Default "Outcome". label_control Character string control group label. Default "Control". label_intervention Character string intervention group label. Default "Intervention". title Character string plot title. Default NULL (title). subtitle Character string plot subtitle. Default NULL (subtitle). title_align Character string specifying title alignment. Options: "left" (default), "center"/"centre", "right". xlim Numeric vector length 2 specifying x-axis limits. Default NULL (auto-scaled). x_breaks Numeric vector specifying custom x-axis break points. Default NULL (uses measure-specific defaults). add_rope Logical indicating whether add ROPE (Region Practical Equivalence). Default FALSE. rope_value Numeric vector length 2 specifying ROPE range, single value symmetric range around null. Default NULL (uses Kruschke's recommendations: /HR/RR/IRR = c(0.9, 1.1), SMD = c(-0.1, 0.1), MD requires specification). rope_color Color ROPE shading. Default transparent grey. shrinkage_output Character string specifying shrinkage visualization. Options: "density" (default) "pointinterval". null_value Numeric value specifying x-axis value null line. Default NULL (uses measure-specific defaults). color_palette Character vector colors plot. Default NULL. color_study_posterior_null_left Color left side null study posteriors. Default "deepskyblue". color_study_posterior_null_right Color right side null study posteriors. Default "violet". color_study_posterior Color study posterior densities. Default "dodgerblue". color_study_posterior_outline Color study posterior outlines. Default "blue". color_overall_posterior Color overall posterior. Default "blue". color_shrinkage_pointinterval Color shrinkage point intervals (used shrinkage_output = "pointinterval"). Default "purple". color_shrinkage_outline Color shrinkage plot outlines. Default "purple". color_shrinkage_fill Color shrinkage plot fill. Default NULL. split_color_by_null Logical. TRUE, posterior densities split coloured based whether values fall null value. color_favours_control Colour used density regions favouring control group split_color_by_null = TRUE. color_favours_intervention Colour used density regions favouring intervention group split_color_by_null = TRUE. add_arm_labels Logical indicating whether display "Favours Control" / "Favours Intervention" labels density plot. Default TRUE. reverse_arm_labels Logical indicating whether swap positions "Favours Control" "Favours Intervention" labels. Default FALSE. add_pred Logical indicating whether add prediction interval row beneath Pooled Effect. Default FALSE. add_pred_subgroup Logical indicating whether add prediction interval rows subgroup subgroup = TRUE. FALSE (default), prediction row added overall pooled effect. Ignored subgroup = FALSE. pred_output Character string specifying visualisation prediction interval row. Options: "density" (default) "pointinterval". color_pred_posterior Color prediction interval density fill. Default "orange". color_pred_outline Color prediction interval density outline. Default "darkorange2". color_pred_pointinterval Color prediction interval point interval. Default "orange". plot_width Numeric value specifying relative width plot component. Default 4. add_rob Logical indicating whether add Risk Bias assessment. Default FALSE. rob_tool Character string specifying RoB tool. Options: "rob2" (default). add_rob_legend Logical indicating whether add RoB legend. Default FALSE. exclude_high_rob Logical indicating whether exclude high risk bias studies refit model. Default FALSE. re_min_k Optional numeric. number studies (sub)group threshold, model downgraded common-effect refit. Overrides re_min_k stored original model's call arguments. Default NULL (uses value original fit, ). incl_shrinkage Logical. TRUE (default), shrinkage (posterior) effect column shown alongside observed effect column. Set FALSE display observed effect column. font Character string specifying font family use throughout plot. Default NULL (uses system defaults).

title: Create Bayesian Forest Plot for Meta-Analysis — forest
text: patchwork object containing complete forest plot study information table, density plots, effect size table.

what: Create a Funnel Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — funnel_plot
title: Create a Funnel Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — funnel_plot
text: Creates publication-ready funnel plot meta-analysis, displaying study-level effect sizes precision (standard error). plot aesthetic matches bayesfoRest package style.

title: Create a Funnel Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — funnel_plot
code: funnel_plot(   model,   data,   estimand,   studyvar = NULL,   year = NULL,   c_n = NULL,   i_n = NULL,   c_event = NULL,   i_event = NULL,   c_mean = NULL,   i_mean = NULL,   c_sd = NULL,   i_sd = NULL,   c_time = NULL,   i_time = NULL,   subgroup = FALSE,   subgroup_var = NULL,   contour_lines = TRUE,   contour_alpha = 0.15,   pooled_line = c("posterior", "fixed", "none"),   color_points = "dodgerblue",   color_points_outline = "blue",   color_pooled = "blue",   color_null = "black",   color_contour = "grey50",   point_size = 3,   xlim = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   null_value = NULL,   add_null_line = FALSE,   label_studies = FALSE,   label_size = 3,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   title_align = "left",   font = NULL )

title: Create a Funnel Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — funnel_plot
text: model fitted bayesma object (class 'bayesma'). data data frame containing study data used meta-analysis. estimand Character string specifying effect measure. Must one : "" (Odds Ratio), "HR" (Hazard Ratio), "RR" (Risk Ratio), "IRR" (Incidence Rate Ratio), "MD" (Mean Difference), "SMD" (Standardized Mean Difference), "RD", "ARR", "ATE", "ATT", "CATE" (marginal estimands). studyvar Column name containing study identifiers/authors. Default NULL. year Column name containing publication years. Default NULL. c_n Column name containing control group sample sizes. i_n Column name containing intervention group sample sizes. c_event Column name containing control group event counts. i_event Column name containing intervention group event counts. c_mean Column name containing control group means. i_mean Column name containing intervention group means. c_sd Column name containing control group standard deviations. i_sd Column name containing intervention group standard deviations. c_time Column name containing control group time periods. i_time Column name containing intervention group time periods. subgroup Logical indicating whether colour points subgroup. Default FALSE. subgroup_var Character string. Name variable data use subgroup colouring. contour_lines Logical indicating whether add significance contour lines (p = 0.10, 0.05, 0.01). Default TRUE. contour_alpha Numeric. Alpha transparency contour shading. Default 0.15. pooled_line Character string specifying pooled estimate line style. Options: "posterior" (default, uses posterior median), "fixed" (uses fixed/common effect), "none" (pooled line). color_points Color study points. Default "dodgerblue". color_points_outline Color study point outlines. Default "blue". color_pooled Color pooled effect line. Default "blue". color_null Color null effect line. Default "black". color_contour Color significance contour fills. Default "grey50". point_size Numeric. Size study points. Default 3. xlim Numeric vector length 2 specifying x-axis limits. Default NULL (auto-scaled). x_breaks Numeric vector specifying custom x-axis break points. Default NULL. null_value Numeric. X-axis value null line. Default NULL (measure-specific). add_null_line Logical. Whether draw vertical line null value. Default FALSE. label_studies Logical indicating whether label study points. Default FALSE. label_size Numeric. Size study labels. Default 3. title Character string plot title. Default NULL. subtitle Character string plot subtitle. Default NULL. title_align Character string specifying title alignment. Options: "left" (default), "center"/"centre", "right". font Character string specifying font family. Default NULL.

title: Create a Funnel Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — funnel_plot
text: ggplot object containing funnel plot.

what: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
text: Tests whether reported mean mathematically possible given sample size number decimal places reported. Applies data measured integer scale (e.g., Likert items, counts).

title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
code: grim_test(mean_value, n, decimals = NULL, tolerance = 1e-06)

title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
text: mean_value Numeric. reported mean. n Integer. sample size. decimals Integer. Number decimal places reported mean. NULL (default), inferred reported value. tolerance Numeric. Rounding tolerance comparison (default 1e-6).

title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
text: list components: consistent Logical. TRUE mean GRIM-consistent. mean_value tested mean. n sample size. decimals Number decimal places used.

title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
text: GRIM test (Brown & Heathers, 2017) checks whether reported mean integer data consistent reported sample size. example, N = 20, mean must multiple 1/20 = 0.05. reported mean 3.47 impossible. implements INSPECT-SR check 4.8.

title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
text: Brown NJL, Heathers JAJ (2017). GRIM test: simple technique detects numerous anomalies reporting results psychology. Social Psychological Personality Science, 8(4), 363-369.

title: GRIM Test (Granularity-Related Inconsistency of Means) — grim_test
code: # Possible mean: 52/20 = 2.60 grim_test(2.60, n = 20) #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $mean_value #> [1] 2.6 #>  #> $n #> [1] 20 #>  #> $decimals #> [1] 1 #>   # Impossible mean: no integer sum / 20 = 2.47 grim_test(2.47, n = 20) #> $consistent #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $mean_value #> [1] 2.47 #>  #> $n #> [1] 20 #>  #> $decimals #> [1] 2 #>

text: data inspect_sr object data frame columns Study, D1, D2, D3, D4, Overall. sort_studies_by "study" (default, alphabetical) "overall" (severity). add_legend Logical. Add legend panel (default FALSE). incl_checks Logical. TRUE, expand table show every INSPECT-SR item (1.1, 1.2, …, 4.11) grouped domain spanners, per-domain "Overall" final study-level "Overall". Requires data inspect_sr object. Default FALSE. font Character. Font family (NULL = default). title, subtitle Character. Optional. title_align "left" (default), "center"/"centre", "right".

title: Create INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Plot — inspect_plot
text: patchwork object.

what: Run the INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Assessment — inspect_sr
title: Run the INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Assessment — inspect_sr
text: Takes data frame one row per study runs automated Domain 4 checks (Carlisle's test, participant-number consistency, GRIM, p-value verification). Manual items (D1, D2, D3, non-automated D4 items) read straight input. Domain-level overall judgements derived per INSPECT-SR guidance (overall = severe domain).

title: Run the INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Assessment — inspect_sr
code: inspect_sr(   data,   studyvar = study,   bayes = FALSE,   prior_prob_trustworthy = 0.9,   pvalue_tolerance = 0.01,   carlisle_method = "fisher",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Assessment — inspect_sr
text: data data frame tibble one row per study. See Expected columns package vignette, bundled inspect_sr_example dataset exact layout. studyvar Unquoted column name identifying study (tidyeval). Defaults study. bayes Logical. FALSE (default) produces frequentist pass/fail judgements. TRUE produces Bayes factors posterior probability trustworthiness. prior_prob_trustworthy Numeric (0, 1). Prior probability study trustworthy, used bayes = TRUE (default 0.90). pvalue_tolerance Numeric. Tolerance frequentist p-value check (default 0.01). carlisle_method "fisher" (default) "ks" — see carlisle_test(). verbose Logical. Print summary console (default TRUE).

title: Run the INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Assessment — inspect_sr
text: bayes = FALSE: object class inspect_sr (data frame columns Study, D1, D2, D3, D4, Overall), per-study details attr(x, "details"). bayes = TRUE: object class bayes_inspect_sr (data frame columns Study, Prior, Posterior, Combined_BF, Interpretation), individual Bayes factors attr(x, "details").

title: Run the INSPECT-SR Trustworthiness Assessment — inspect_sr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(inspect_sr_example)  # Frequentist res <- inspect_sr(inspect_sr_example, studyvar = study)  # Bayesian res_bayes <- inspect_sr(inspect_sr_example, studyvar = study, bayes = TRUE) } # }

what: Example INSPECT-SR Dataset: EEG-Guided Anaesthesia and Delirium — inspect_sr_example
title: Example INSPECT-SR Dataset: EEG-Guided Anaesthesia and Delirium — inspect_sr_example
text: simulated eight-trial systematic review used demonstrate full INSPECT-SR workflow. eight trials hand-crafted span range trustworthiness profiles exercise every automated check.

title: Example INSPECT-SR Dataset: EEG-Guided Anaesthesia and Delirium — inspect_sr_example
code: inspect_sr_example

title: Example INSPECT-SR Dataset: EEG-Guided Anaesthesia and Delirium — inspect_sr_example
text: tibble one row per study. Every INSPECT-SR item column expected layout immediately obvious. Variable-length inputs automated Domain 4 checks (Table 1 baselines reported test statistics) stored list-columns. study Character. Study identifier (must unique). n_randomised_int, n_randomised_ctrl, n_randomised_total, n_analysed_int, n_analysed_ctrl, n_lost_int, n_lost_ctrl Integer participant-flow columns feeding check 4.6. d1_1..d1_3, d2_1..d2_5, d3_1..d3_2, d4_1..d4_11 Character. Manual judgement INSPECT-SR item. Allowed values: "concerns", "concerns", "Serious concerns", NA. four automated items (d4_3, d4_6, d4_8, d4_9) left NA filled inspect_sr(). d1_1_comment..d4_11_comment Character. Optional free-text comment attached judgement. baseline List-column. element data frame Table 1 variables columns variable, mean_int, sd_int, mean_ctrl, sd_ctrl, p_value, integer_scale. Feeds GRIM (4.8) Carlisle (4.3). statistics List-column. element data frame reported tests columns test_type, statistic, df, df2, reported_p, context. Feeds p-value check (4.9). outcome_estimate, outcome_ci_lower, outcome_ci_upper, outcome_se, outcome_log_scale Primary outcome (odds ratio delirium incidence) — values log scale.

title: Example INSPECT-SR Dataset: EEG-Guided Anaesthesia and Delirium — inspect_sr_example

title: Example INSPECT-SR Dataset: EEG-Guided Anaesthesia and Delirium — inspect_sr_example

title: Plot Method for bayesma_coef_evidence Objects — metareg_mod_plot
text: x bayesma_coef_evidence object (created coefficient_evidence()). model bayesma_metareg model. Required extract posterior draws density slabs. include_intercept Logical. Include intercept plot (default: FALSE). show_null_range Logical. Show null range region available (default: TRUE). null_value Numeric. Value null reference line (default: 0). output Character. Either "density" (default) density slabs "pointinterval" point + interval display. split_color_by_null Logical. TRUE, posterior densities split coloured based whether values fall null value (default: FALSE). color_posterior Color coefficient posterior densities (default: "dodgerblue"). color_posterior_outline Color posterior outlines ( default: "blue"). color_favours_positive Colour density regions > null split_color_by_null = TRUE (default: "dodgerblue"). color_favours_negative Colour density regions < null split_color_by_null = TRUE (default: "firebrick"). color_null_range Color null range shading (default: "grey50"). color_pointinterval Color point intervals output = "pointinterval" (default: "blue"). xlim Numeric vector length 2 specifying x-axis limits. Default NULL (auto-scaled). x_breaks Numeric vector specifying custom x-axis break points. Default NULL (auto). xlab Character. X-axis label (default: "Coefficient Estimate"). title Character. Plot title (default: "Meta-Regression Coefficients"). subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default shows null range provided. add_table Logical. Add table coefficient summaries right (default: TRUE). table_width Numeric. Relative width table vs plot (default: 0.4). font Character. Font family text elements (default: NULL). ... Additional arguments (unused).

title: Plot Method for bayesma_coef_evidence Objects — metareg_mod_plot

title: Create posterior plots for Bayesian meta-analysis — overall_plot
text: model bayesma object. estimand Character string effect measure marginal estimand. Relative-effect: "", "HR", "RR", "IRR", "MD", "SMD". Marginal: "RD"/"ARR", "ATE", "ATT", "CATE". marginal estimands pooled posterior drawn model$marginal$draws. add_tau Logical, whether include heterogeneity (tau) plot. add_mu_prior Logical, whether include prior distribution mu. add_tau_prior Logical, whether include prior distribution tau. add_pred Logical, whether include prediction distribution plot. color_pred_posterior Color prediction posterior distribution. color_pred_posterior_outline Outline color prediction posterior distribution. null_value Numeric, null value effect (default get_measure_properties). null_range Numeric vector length 2, range null region (e.g., c(0.9, 1.1)). add_null_range Logical, whether add shaded null range region. color_null_range Color null range shading. label_control Character string control group label. label_intervention Character string intervention group label. title Plot title. subtitle Plot subtitle. title_align Alignment title ("left", "center", "right"). mu_xlim Numeric vector length 2 mu plot x-axis limits. tau_xlim Numeric vector length 2 tau plot x-axis limits. pred_xlim Numeric vector length 2 prediction plot x-axis limits. x_breaks Numeric vector x-axis breaks mu plot. tau_breaks Numeric vector x-axis breaks tau plot. pred_breaks Numeric vector x-axis breaks prediction plot. color_overall_posterior Color overall posterior distribution. color_overall_posterior_outline Outline color posterior distribution. split_color_by_null Logical, whether split posterior color null value. color_favours_control Color region favoring control. color_favours_intervention Color region favoring intervention. tau_posterior_color Base color tau posterior. tau_posterior_outline Outline color tau posterior. tau_slab_scale Numeric, scaling factor tau slab height relative mu (default 0.7). font Font family text elements. plot_arrangement Character, "vertical" (stacked) "horizontal" (side side).

what: Posterior and Prior Predictive Checks for bayesma Objects — pp_check
title: Posterior and Prior Predictive Checks for bayesma Objects — pp_check
text: Compares observed data replicated datasets drawn either posterior predictive distribution (standard use) prior predictive distribution (model fitted sample_prior = TRUE). Posterior predictive check (sample_prior = FALSE, default): draws y_rep p(y_rep | y). Agreement y y_rep indicates fitted model can reproduce observed data. Prior predictive check (sample_prior = TRUE): draws y_rep p(y_rep), integrating prior without conditioning data. tool prior elicitation sanity-checking — verifying prior place substantial mass data values impossible implausible given domain knowledge, data seen.

what: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
title: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
text: Runs four-step exploratory/preliminary data analysis workflow meta-analysis dependent effect sizes described Pustejovsky, Zhang, & Tipton (2026).

title: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
code: primed(   data,   es_col,   se_col,   study_col,   sample_col = NULL,   n_col = NULL,   moderators = NULL,   es_type = c("SMD", "correlation"),   df_col = NULL,   rho_values = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9),   fence_multiplier = 3 )

title: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
text: data data frame containing meta-analytic database. es_col Character. Name column containing effect size estimates. se_col Character. Name column containing standard errors. study_col Character. Name column identifying studies. sample_col Character. Name column identifying samples within studies. NULL (default), assumes one sample per study. n_col Character. Name column containing total sample sizes. NULL, sample size plots skipped. moderators Character vector column names examine potential moderators. NULL (default), moderator analysis performed. es_type Character. Type effect size: "SMD" (standardized mean difference) "correlation". Affects scaled SEs weights computed. Default "SMD". df_col Character. Name column containing degrees freedom (used scaled SE calculation es_type = "SMD"). NULL, scaled SEs computed. rho_values Numeric vector. Assumed within-sample correlations ISC weight calculations. Default c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9). fence_multiplier Numeric. Multiplier IQR outlier fences effect size density plots. Default 3 (following Tukey's conventions described paper).

title: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
text: named list elements: summary list summary statistics database. plots named list ggplot objects workflow step. tables named list summary tables (tibbles).

title: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
text: PRIMED workflow proceeds four steps: Data structure: Counts observations level describes dependence structure (effects per sample, samples per study, sample size distributions). Moderators: Examines marginal distributions, missingness, hierarchical (within- vs -sample) structure covariates. Standard errors & weights: Inspects SE distributions within samples, computes scaled SEs (SMDs), calculates ISC weights varying assumed correlations. Effect size distribution: Visualises marginal sample-level densities outlier fences, produces hierarchical forest plot dependent effect sizes.

title: PRIMED: Preliminary Investigation of Meta-analytic Databases — primed
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- primed(   data = my_meta_data,   es_col = "g",   se_col = "se",   study_col = "study",   sample_col = "sample_id",   n_col = "n_total",   moderators = c("intervention_type", "mean_age", "pct_female"),   es_type = "SMD",   df_col = "df" )  # View all step-1 plots results$plots$step1_es_per_sample results$plots$step1_samples_per_study  # Access summary statistics results$summary } # }

Prior model weight bias-prior mixture. ... title: Prior distribution constructors — priors
text: mean Numeric. Mean normal half-normal prior. sd Numeric. Standard deviation normal half-normal prior. location Numeric. Location Cauchy Student-t prior. scale Numeric. Scale Cauchy, Student-t, scaled inverse chi-squared prior. df Numeric. Degrees freedom Student-t scaled inverse chi-squared prior. rate Numeric. Rate parameter exponential prior. lower, upper Numeric. Endpoints uniform prior. alpha Numeric (vector Dirichlet, scalar Beta). Shape parameter(s). beta Numeric. Second shape parameter Beta prior. eta Numeric. Shape parameter LKJ correlation prior. eta = 1 uniform; eta > 1 concentrates toward identity; eta < 1 concentrates toward perfect correlation.

title: Compute R-squared for Meta-Regression — r_squared
text: Calculates proportion -study heterogeneity explained moderators. Requires fitting model without moderators comparison.

title: Compute R-squared for Meta-Regression — r_squared
code: r_squared(   object,   null_model = NULL,   method = c("tau", "tau_sq"),   summary = c("median", "mean") )

title: Compute R-squared for Meta-Regression — r_squared
text: object bayesma_reg object (model moderators). null_model Optional. bayesma object without moderators. NULL, function fit one automatically using stored call arguments. method Character. Method computing R². Options: "tau": Based reduction tau (default). R² = 1 - (tau_reg / tau_null) "tau_sq": Based reduction tau². R² = 1 - (tau²_reg / tau²_null summary Character. summarize posterior. "median" (default) "mean".

title: Compute R-squared for Meta-Regression — r_squared
text: list : R2: Point estimate R² R2_ci: 95% credible interval R² R2_draws: Full posterior distribution R² tau_null: Tau null model tau_reg: Tau regression model method: Method used

title: Compute R-squared for Meta-Regression — r_squared
text: R² meta-regression represents proportion -study variance (heterogeneity) explained moderators. calculated : $$R^2 = 1 - \frac{\tau^2_{regression}}{\tau^2_{null}}$$ \(\tau^2_{null}\) heterogeneity model without moderators, \(\tau^2_{regression}\) residual heterogeneity accounting moderators. Note R² can negative moderators explain heterogeneity (model moderators fits worse due overfitting weak priors).

title: Compute R-squared for Meta-Regression — r_squared
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fit meta-regression fit_reg <- meta_reg(data, study = "author", yi = "yi", vi = "vi",                     mods = ~ year + quality)  # Compute R² r2 <- r_squared(fit_reg) print(r2)  # With pre-fitted null model fit_null <- bayesma(data, study = "author", ...) r2 <- r_squared(fit_reg, null_model = fit_null) } # }

what: Robust Bayesian Model Averaging for Meta-Analysis — robma
title: Robust Bayesian Model Averaging for Meta-Analysis — robma
text: Fits Robust Bayesian Meta-Analysis (RoBMA) model using Bayesian model averaging across models without effect, heterogeneity, publication bias. Use stan_code(model) inspect generated Stan programs fitting.

title: Robust Bayesian Model Averaging for Meta-Analysis — robma
code: robma(   data,   studyvar,   event_ctrl = NULL,   event_int = NULL,   mean_ctrl = NULL,   mean_int = NULL,   sd_ctrl = NULL,   sd_int = NULL,   n_ctrl = NULL,   n_int = NULL,   likelihood = c("binomial", "gaussian", "poisson"),   priors_effect = NULL,   priors_effect_null = NULL,   priors_heterogeneity = NULL,   priors_heterogeneity_null = NULL,   priors_bias = NULL,   priors_bias_null = NULL,   rescale_priors = 1,   method = c("bridge", "ss"),   bias_indicator = c("bias_corrected", "pet_peese", "selection_weight"),   null_range = NULL,   b_prior = NULL,   p_bias_prior = NULL,   p_cutoffs = c(0.025, 0.05),   parallel = FALSE,   chains = 4,   iter_warmup = 1000,   iter_sampling = 1000,   adapt_delta = 0.95,   seed = 1234,   quiet = FALSE,   custom_model = NULL,   custom_data = NULL,   format = TRUE,   ... )

title: Robust Bayesian Model Averaging for Meta-Analysis — robma
text: data data frame one row per study. studyvar Character. Column name study identifier. event_ctrl, event_int Character. Column names event counts (binomial / Poisson likelihoods). mean_ctrl, mean_int, sd_ctrl, sd_int Character. Column names arm means SDs (Gaussian likelihood). n_ctrl, n_int Character. Column names arm sample sizes. likelihood Character. One "binomial", "gaussian", "poisson". priors_effect, priors_effect_null, priors_heterogeneity, priors_heterogeneity_null, priors_bias, priors_bias_null Lists prior objects effect, heterogeneity, publication-bias components (alternative null). NULL, RoBMA defaults used. rescale_priors Numeric. Scale factor applied default priors. Default 1. method Character. "bridge" (default) uses bridge sampling across full model grid; "ss" uses single spike--slab Stan model. bias_indicator Character. Spike--slab bias mechanism: "bias_corrected", "pet_peese", "selection_weight". null_range Numeric vector length 2 giving null range log scale (e.g., c(-0.1, 0.1) log ). Effects within range considered practically equivalent zero. Defaults NULL (point null exactly zero). /RR, c(-0.1, 0.1) corresponds /RR [0.905, 1.105]. b_prior Prior b slope spike--slab bias correction. p_bias_prior Prior bias inclusion probability. p_cutoffs Numeric vector one-sided p-value cutoffs selection-weight models. Default c(0.025, 0.05). parallel Logical. Fit bridgesampling grid parallel. chains, iter_warmup, iter_sampling, adapt_delta, seed MCMC settings. quiet Logical. Suppress per-step progress messages. custom_model Optional Stan program(s) override code generation. method = "bridge", named list character scalars keyed model label. method = "ss", single character scalar. custom_data Optional Stan data overrides merged onto auto-built data list(s). shape conventions custom_model. format Logical. TRUE (default), auto-format generated Stan programs stanc --auto-format. ... Additional arguments passed cmdstanr::CmdStanModel$sample().

title: Run RoBMA Models Across Multiple Prior Specifications — robma_sensitivity
text: data data frame containing study data. priors named list prior specifications. element must list least mu_prior (optionally) tau_prior, may include name used display. Example: list(     prior_1 = list(name = "Vague", mu_prior = normal(0, 10), tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 1)),     prior_2 = list(name = "Regularising", mu_prior = normal(0, 1), tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 0.5))   ) robma_template Either: fitted RoBMA object (class bayesma_robma) stored $meta$call_args use template refitting across priors, NULL (default), case robma_args must provided robma_args list arguments pass robma() robma_template NULL. Must include required arguments except priors_effect priors_heterogeneity, set priors argument. parallel Logical. TRUE, uses parallel processing refits. workers Optional integer. Number parallel workers. seed Logical. TRUE (default), uses parallel-safe seeding. .progress Logical. TRUE, displays progress bar.

title: Run RoBMA Models Across Multiple Prior Specifications — robma_sensitivity
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Define priors for sensitivity analysis priors <- list(   vague = list(     name = "Vague",     mu_prior = normal(0, 10),     tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 1)   ),   informative = list(     name = "Informative",     mu_prior = normal(0, 0.5),     tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 0.3)   ) )  # Option 1: Using a template RoBMA fit robma_sens <- robma_sensitivity(   data = my_data,   priors = priors,   robma_template = my_robma_fit,   parallel = TRUE )  # Option 2: Specifying robma arguments directly robma_sens <- robma_sensitivity(   data = my_data,   priors = priors,   robma_args = list(     studyvar = "study_id",     event_ctrl = "events_c",     event_int = "events_i",     n_ctrl = "n_c",     n_int = "n_i",     likelihood = "binomial"   ),   parallel = TRUE )  # Attach to bayesma model model <- attach_robma_sensitivity(model, robma_sens)  # Now sensitivity_plot can use the pre-computed RoBMA fits sensitivity_plot(model, data, priors, estimand = "OR", incl_robma = TRUE) } # }

title: Run RoBMA Models Across Multiple Prior Specifications — run_robma_sensitivity
text: data data frame containing study data. priors named list prior specifications. element must list least mu_prior (optionally) tau_prior, may include name used display. Example: list(     prior_1 = list(name = "Vague", mu_prior = normal(0, 10), tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 1)),     prior_2 = list(name = "Regularising", mu_prior = normal(0, 1), tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 0.5))   ) robma_template Either: fitted RoBMA object (class bayesma_robma) stored $meta$call_args use template refitting across priors, NULL (default), case robma_args must provided robma_args list arguments pass robma() robma_template NULL. Must include required arguments except priors_effect priors_heterogeneity, set priors argument. parallel Logical. TRUE, uses parallel processing refits. workers Optional integer. Number parallel workers. seed Logical. TRUE (default), uses parallel-safe seeding. .progress Logical. TRUE, displays progress bar.

title: Run RoBMA Models Across Multiple Prior Specifications — run_robma_sensitivity
text: bayesma_robma_sensitivity object: named list RoBMA fits keyed prior IDs, additional metadata $meta.

title: Run RoBMA Models Across Multiple Prior Specifications — run_robma_sensitivity
text: returned object can attached bayesma model using attach_robma_sensitivity() passed directly sensitivity_plot().

title: Run RoBMA Models Across Multiple Prior Specifications — run_robma_sensitivity
code: # Define priors for sensitivity analysis priors <- list(   vague = list(     name = "Vague",     mu_prior = normal(0, 10),     tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 1)   ),   informative = list(     name = "Informative",     mu_prior = normal(0, 0.5),     tau_prior = half_cauchy(0, 0.3)   ) )  # Option 1: Using a template RoBMA fit robma_sens <- run_robma_sensitivity(   data = my_data,   priors = priors,   robma_template = my_robma_fit,   parallel = TRUE ) #> Error: object 'my_robma_fit' not found  # Option 2: Specifying robma arguments directly robma_sens <- run_robma_sensitivity(   data = my_data,   priors = priors,   robma_args = list(     study = "study_id",     event_ctrl = "events_c",     event_int = "events_i",     n_ctrl = "n_c",     n_int = "n_i",     likelihood = "binomial"   ),   parallel = TRUE ) #>  #> ── Running RoBMA sensitivity analysis ── #>  #> ℹ Fitting 2 RoBMA models across prior specifications #> ℹ Running in parallel mode with auto workers #> Error: object 'my_data' not found  # Attach to bayesma model model <- attach_robma_sensitivity(model, robma_sens) #> Error: object 'model' not found  # Now sensitivity_plot can use the pre-computed RoBMA fits sensitivity_plot(model, data, priors, measure = "OR", incl_robma = TRUE) #> Error: object 'model' not found

text: x bayesma_sensitivity_plot object. show Logical. Whether display posterior probability columns. range Numeric vector length 2 giving null/ROPE range, NULL use default model's estimand. color_null_range Colour used shade null range. title, subtitle Character. Plot titles. align Title alignment: "left", "center", "right". xlim Numeric vector length 2. New x-axis limits. x_breaks Optional numeric vector x-axis break locations. plot_width Positive numeric. Relative width density panel.

what: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — sensitivity_plot
title: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — sensitivity_plot
text: Creates sensitivity analysis visualisation showing meta-analytic estimates vary across different model strategies user-specified priors. RoBMA results, models must pre-computed using run_robma_sensitivity() attached bayesma object calling function.

title: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — sensitivity_plot
code: sensitivity_plot(   model,   data,   priors,   estimand,   prior_order = NULL,   model_order = NULL,   rob_var = NULL,   exclude_high_rob = FALSE,   incl_common_effect = FALSE,   incl_random_effect = TRUE,   incl_bias_corrected = FALSE,   incl_selection_copas = FALSE,   incl_selection_weight = FALSE,   incl_pet_peese = FALSE,   incl_robust = FALSE,   incl_robma = FALSE,   parallel = FALSE,   workers = NULL,   seed = TRUE,   add_probs = FALSE,   null_value = NULL,   null_range = NULL,   add_null_range = FALSE,   color_null_range = "#77bb41",   label_control = "Control",   label_intervention = "Intervention",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   title_align = "left",   xlim = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   color_palette = NULL,   color_overall_posterior = "dodgerblue",   color_overall_posterior_outline = "blue",   split_color_by_null = FALSE,   color_favours_control = "firebrick",   color_favours_intervention = "dodgerblue",   plot_width = 4,   font = NULL )

title: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — sensitivity_plot
text: model fitted bayesma object. RoBMA results, must $robma_sensitivity attached via attach_robma_sensitivity(). data data frame containing study data used fit model. priors named list prior specifications. element must list least mu_prior (optionally) tau_prior, may include name used display. estimand Effect measure marginal estimand. Relative-effect: "", "HR", "RR", "IRR", "MD", "SMD". Marginal: "RD"/"ARR", "ATE", "ATT", "CATE". prior_order Optional character vector specifying display order priors. contain names (IDs) priors desired order, e.g., c("vague", "weak_reg", "informative"). NULL (default), priors displayed order appear priors list. model_order Optional. Specifies display order model sections. Can provided unquoted names (using rlang) character vector. Valid values match incl_* argument names (without incl_ prefix): common_effect, random_effect, bias_corrected, selection_copas, selection_weight, pet_peese, robust, robma. RoBMA, use robma include conditional model-averaged estimates (conditional first), robma_conditional / robma_model_averaged separately. Example: model_order = c(common_effect, random_effect, pet_peese, robma) NULL (default), sections displayed default order. rob_var Optional. Name risk--bias variable (unquoted). exclude_high_rob Logical. TRUE, runs "Excluding High RoB" section. incl_common_effect, incl_random_effect, incl_bias_corrected, incl_selection_copas, incl_selection_weight, incl_pet_peese, incl_robust Logical. model strategy sections include. incl_robma Logical. TRUE, include RoBMA section. Requires model$robma_sensitivity present (see Details). parallel Logical. TRUE, uses parallel processing non-RoBMA refits. workers Optional integer. Number parallel workers. seed Logical. TRUE (default), uses parallel-safe seeding. add_probs Logical. Add probability columns results table. null_value, null_range, add_null_range, color_null_range ROPE settings. label_control, label_intervention Group labels plot. title, subtitle, title_align Title settings. xlim, x_breaks Density axis settings. color_palette, color_overall_posterior, color_overall_posterior_outline Colour settings. split_color_by_null Logical. TRUE, colour posterior split null value using color_favours_control color_favours_intervention. color_favours_control Colour side posterior favouring control. color_favours_intervention Colour side favouring intervention. plot_width Width ratio density plot section. font Optional font family.

title: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — sensitivity_plot
text: bayesma_sensitivity_plot object (patchwork combining tables plots).

title: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Plot for Bayesian Meta-Analysis — sensitivity_plot
text: include RoBMA sensitivity plot, must pre-compute RoBMA fits:   separation allows computationally expensive RoBMA fitting done cached, rather re-run every time plot generated.
code: # Step 1: Run RoBMA sensitivity analysis robma_sens <- run_robma_sensitivity(   data = my_data,   priors = my_priors,   robma_template = my_robma_fit,   parallel = TRUE )  # Step 2: Attach to bayesma model model <- attach_robma_sensitivity(model, robma_sens)  # Step 3: Create sensitivity plot with RoBMA sensitivity_plot(model, data, priors, estimand = "OR", incl_robma = TRUE)

title: Verify a Reported P-Value — verify_pvalue
text: Recalculates p-value reported test statistic degrees freedom, compares reported p-value.

title: Verify a Reported P-Value — verify_pvalue
code: verify_pvalue(   test_type = c("t", "z", "chi_sq", "f"),   statistic,   df = NULL,   reported_p,   alternative = "two.sided",   tolerance = 0.01 )

title: Verify a Reported P-Value — verify_pvalue
text: test_type Character. One "t", "z", "chi_sq", "f". statistic Numeric. reported test statistic. df Numeric. Degrees freedom. F-tests, vector length 2. required z-tests. reported_p Numeric. reported p-value. alternative Character. One "two.sided" (default), "less", "greater". tolerance Numeric. Acceptable absolute difference (default 0.01).

title: Verify a Reported P-Value — verify_pvalue
text: list components: consistent Logical. TRUE p-values match within tolerance. reported_p reported p-value. recalculated_p recalculated p-value. difference Absolute difference. test_type test type used. statistic test statistic.

title: Verify a Reported P-Value — verify_pvalue
text: Implements INSPECT-SR check 4.9.

title: Verify a Reported P-Value — verify_pvalue
code: # Consistent: chi-squared = 3.84, df = 1, p = 0.05 verify_pvalue("chi_sq", statistic = 3.84, df = 1, reported_p = 0.05) #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $reported_p #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $recalculated_p #> [1] 0.05004352 #>  #> $difference #> [1] 4.352125e-05 #>  #> $test_type #> [1] "chi_sq" #>  #> $statistic #> [1] 3.84 #>
